text: Entering this space, with its glass walls and vibrant neon, I feel both curious and dislocated—as if the world is tilting. The second room, filled with signs of research and development, hints at human ingenuity and the intersection between technology and thought. I sense that I am on the threshold of innovation, where the past and future intermingle. It is an invigorating, almost electric sensation, but with an undertone of uncertainty about the unknown.
